campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: Campbell City Council met to discuss several items including accepting a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help pay for youth recreation scholarships. The council also discussed extending a temporary local rule and moving forward with plans to build a bicycle and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue, including submitting grant applications to help pay for it.
keyTopics: $4,000 donation to youth scholarship fund from Water Tower Kitchen
Extension of temporary ordinance number 2326
Bicycle and pedestrian bridge planned over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue
Grant application submission for bridge project

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 4, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council approved funding decisions for local events and grants. They decided which organizations would receive money through the secured funding program, approved how much funding will go to competitive event grants for the year, and made updates to the rules for community events and street closures.
keyTopics: Approved specific organizations to receive secured grant funding
Set total funding amount for competitive event grants this fiscal year
Updated rules for community events and street closure permits

san-jose.title: San José City Council — March 25, 2026
san-jose.summary: This San José City Council meeting on March 25, 2026 provided information about how residents can watch the meeting through multiple platforms including cable TV, online streaming, and phone dial-in. The meeting offered live translations in over 50 languages for accessibility.
keyTopics: Multiple ways to watch the meeting (cable, YouTube, Zoom, phone)
Live translations available in 50+ languages
Meeting accessible via Cable Channel 26 and online platforms
No public comment period during this session

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 25, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council cancelled their zoning hearing that was supposed to happen on March 25, 2026. They've rescheduled it for April 8, 2026 instead, so if you had plans to speak about a zoning issue or property matter, you'll need to show up on the new date.
keyTopics: March 25 zoning hearing cancelled
Rescheduled to April 8, 2026
Administrative zoning decisions delayed

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review applications for federal Community Development Block Grant funding, which helps pay for local housing and support programs. The group also approved meeting minutes from their previous session in February.
keyTopics: Federal grant funding applications for housing and community services
Review of organizations seeking CDBG money for local projects
Approval of February 25 meeting minutes

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council focused on helping local businesses grow and succeed. They discussed a program to help businesses that may not have proper licenses get into compliance, while also planning campaigns to encourage residents to shop locally and partnering with private companies to sponsor city events and offer incentives for new retail stores and startups.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program to help non-compliant businesses
Shop Local campaigns for the summer
Private companies sponsoring city events
Tax breaks and incentives for retail and startups
Outreach to shopping centers and local businesses

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — March 24, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on March 24, 2026, where they addressed several postponed items and approved a plan to tackle overgrown weeds around the city as part of a county-wide cleanup program. The council also opened public discussion about how federal housing grant money will be spent in the community over the next year.
keyTopics: Weed abatement program - city will remove overgrown weeds on properties
Federal housing grant funds - public input on how $HUD money will be used in 2026-27
Multiple agenda items postponed to future meetings

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026 to approve several items including a proclamation celebrating Women's History Month. The council also approved an agreement with the American Red Cross to use city facilities during disasters and authorized a three-year contract with Insight Public Sector to provide email security and backup services for about $93,400 per year.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
American Red Cross disaster facility agreement
Email security software contract ($93,383/year for 3 years)
City facility usage during emergencies
